dna.description: Atos is a global leader in digital transformation, cybersecurity, and decarbonization services. The company provides end-to-end digital solutions spanning cloud infrastructure, big data and analytics, business applications, cybersecurity, and high-performance computing. Following its restructuring, Atos focuses on mission-critical IT services for defense, healthcare, and public sector clients. Listed on Euronext Paris (ATO), Atos serves clients in 69 countries with deep expertise in sovereign and secure digital infrastructure.
dna.sub_industry: IT Services / Cybersecurity / Digital Transformation / Managed Services
dna.business_model: B2B IT Services + Managed Services + Consulting + Systems Integration
products: Atos Codex (big data and analytics platform)
features: Multi-cloud orchestration and hybrid cloud management
Identity and access management at enterprise scale
AI-driven threat detection and incident response
Digital workplace transformation and device management
SAP S/4HANA migration and managed services
Carbon footprint tracking and decarbonization analytics
target_segments: Olympic and major sporting event organizers
use_cases: Enterprise cybersecurity operations and threat monitoring
Cloud migration and hybrid cloud management
Digital workplace transformation and end-user support
SAP S/4HANA migration and managed services
High-performance computing for weather forecasting and research
Identity and access management for large organizations
dna.pricing_model: Multi-year managed services contracts (3-7 years) with monthly recurring fees. Project-based consulting and systems integration billing. Time-and-materials for professional services. Subscription licensing for Evidian and cybersecurity products.
dna.company_size_signals: 95,000+ employees, operations in 69 countries, revenue of EUR 10.7 billion (2024). Headquartered in Bezons, France. Listed on Euronext Paris (ATO). Official IT partner of the Olympic Games.
social_proof: French Ministry of Armed Forces (sovereign cybersecurity)
European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (HPC)
dna.tone_of_voice: Mission-driven, security-focused, institutional, with emphasis on sovereignty, trust, and critical infrastructure protection

market.market_size_estimate: Global IT services market: ~USD 1.3 trillion (2024). Cybersecurity services market: ~USD 200 billion. Managed cloud services: ~USD 120 billion. HPC market: ~USD 50 billion. Total addressable market for Atos: ~USD 250 billion.
market.growth_trend: IT services market growing at 5-7% CAGR. Cybersecurity services growing at 12-15% CAGR driven by increasing threat landscape and regulatory mandates. Sovereign cloud and defense IT accelerating at 20%+ in Europe. HPC growing at 8-10% CAGR driven by AI and scientific computing.
likely_competitors: Capgemini (French IT services giant with strong consulting and digital transformation)
Accenture (global leader in consulting and technology services)
IBM (enterprise IT services, hybrid cloud, and mainframe legacy)
Tata Consultancy Services (largest Indian IT services company by revenue)
Infosys (Indian IT services with strong digital and cloud capabilities)
Thales (cybersecurity and defense technology, direct competitor in sovereign IT)
Sopra Steria (French IT services focused on consulting and software)
competitive_advantages: Strongest position in European sovereign and defense IT
Bull HPC technology powering European exascale computing
Official Olympic IT partner demonstrating large-scale event operations
Deep public sector relationships across European governments
Combined cybersecurity and managed services offering
market_challenges: Financial restructuring has created client confidence concerns
Indian IT services firms offer lower-cost alternatives
Accenture and Capgemini winning digital transformation budgets
Loss of Worldline spinoff removed high-growth payments business
Talent retention challenges during corporate transformation
customer_pain_points: Growing cybersecurity threats targeting critical infrastructure
Complexity of managing multi-cloud and hybrid cloud environments
Need for sovereign IT infrastructure compliant with European regulations
Legacy IT systems requiring modernization (mainframe, SAP ECC)
Difficulty finding IT service providers with defense-grade security clearances
Rising costs of in-house IT operations and security operations centers
buying_triggers: Cybersecurity incident or breach requiring improved defenses
Government mandate for sovereign cloud or data localization
SAP ECC end-of-life requiring S/4HANA migration
Defense contract requiring NATO or national security clearances
Digital transformation initiative from new CIO or CDO
IT outsourcing contract renewal or competitive rebid
Olympic or major event requiring massive IT infrastructure
market.sales_cycle_estimate: 3-6 months for managed services extensions, 6-12 months for new cybersecurity and cloud managed services contracts, 12-24 months for large government and defense IT outsourcing deals
ideal_customer_characteristics: European organization in defense, public sector, or critical infrastructure
Annual IT budget above USD 10 million
Need for security-cleared IT service provider
Company operating in regulated industry with compliance mandates
Organization with 1,000+ employees requiring managed IT services
Government agency requiring sovereign cloud infrastructure
Company seeking to outsource cybersecurity operations (SOC)
Multi-national European company needing pan-European IT delivery
anti_icp_signals: Small company with fewer than 100 employees (deal size too small)
Startup with no legacy IT infrastructure to manage
Company with fully in-house IT team and no outsourcing appetite
Organization exclusively using US hyperscalers with no sovereignty needs
Business looking only for staff augmentation without managed services
Company in a non-regulated industry with minimal security requirements
Organization outside Europe with no European operations
expansion_opportunities: Sovereign AI infrastructure for European governments
Managed cybersecurity for critical infrastructure (NIS2 compliance)
Quantum computing research and readiness services
Digital twin and simulation for defense applications
Carbon footprint management and ESG technology services
industry_trends: European digital sovereignty driving demand for trusted IT providers
NIS2 directive forcing critical infrastructure cybersecurity investments
SAP S/4HANA mandatory migration creating large project pipeline
Zero Trust architecture becoming enterprise security standard
HPC convergence with AI for scientific and industrial applications
